Statement_Summary: AMS scope limited to applications/modules explicitly listed in AMS agreement; adding new application requires contract scope change
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects support for an application not in the AMS agreement
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm application list before signing AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: APPSolve provides Level 2 and Level 3 support only; Level 1 is client's responsibility unless explicitly contracted
Risk_If_Invalid: Client routes all user queries to APPSolve including basic how-to questions
Customer_Action_Required: Establish internal Level 1 helpdesk before AMS commences

Statement_Summary: Level 2 covers functional config questions / config adjustments / error investigation / security role support / data correction / workflow adjustments / report adjustments
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects Level 2 to include business process redesign and enhancements

Statement_Summary: Level 3 covers technical investigation / Oracle SR management / OIC/BeBanking error investigation / performance diagnosis / complex senior-level issues
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects Level 3 to include new integration development

Statement_Summary: Support limited to Oracle applications configured and delivered by APPSolve; third-party configured apps require separate assessment and onboarding
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to support Oracle module configured by another system integrator
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm implementation source of all applications before AMS onboarding

Statement_Summary: BeBanking AMS only available where Carin Webb (sole BeBanking OIC resource) is available; SPOF noted in CONSULTANT_SKILL_MATRIX.md
Risk_If_Invalid: BeBanking AMS cannot be fulfilled during Carin Webb absence without succession plan
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge BeBanking OIC SPOF; confirm AMS continuity plan

Statement_Summary: Acumatica AMS covers config adjustments / workflow and customisation support within implemented framework / data correction / release advisory / user access where contracted
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects Acumatica AMS to cover new customisation development

Statement_Summary: Standard support hours: 08:00–17:00 SAST Mon–Fri excluding SA public holidays; extended hours and 24/7 available as contracted add-ons at uplift rate
Risk_If_Invalid: Client assumes 24/7 support under standard AMS agreement
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm support hours tier in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: AMS delivered remotely by default; on-site support at separate on-site day rate plus travel; remote-first is APPSolve standard
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects on-site support as standard

Statement_Summary: Monthly support hours allocation defined per contract; hours not used in a month do not roll over
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects unused hours to accumulate across months
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm monthly hours allocation in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: Hours tracked and reported monthly: incidents resolved / SRs completed / hours consumed / hours remaining / CRs / open items
Risk_If_Invalid: Client unaware of hours consumed until invoice arrives

Statement_Summary: APPSolve notifies client when monthly allocation is exhausted; additional hours approved by client in writing at AMS hourly rate; no work beyond allocation without approval
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve continues working beyond monthly allocation without client approval; invoice dispute
Customer_Action_Required: Designate AMS contact authorised to approve additional hours

Statement_Summary: No support on SA public holidays under standard business hours agreement; public holiday support at out-of-hours rate
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects full support on public holidays under standard AMS

Statement_Summary: All support requests must be logged via designated APPSolve support channel (portal / email / agreed ticketing); informal channels acknowledged but must be formally logged
Risk_If_Invalid: Support work done informally without tickets; no audit trail; billing disputes
Customer_Action_Required: Use designated support channel for all requests

Statement_Summary: Telephone support for P1 Critical only; P2/P3/P4 are ticket-first; phone follow-up at APPSolve discretion
Risk_If_Invalid: Client calls consultant directly for all issues bypassing ticket system

Statement_Summary: Designated APPSolve AMS Account Manager / Team Lead is primary escalation point for service delivery concerns / SLA disputes / CR approvals
Risk_If_Invalid: Client escalates directly to Hein Blignaut or Director for routine AMS issues

Statement_Summary: Client designates primary AMS contact authorised to log requests / approve CRs / receive reports; APPSolve only takes direction from designated contact
Risk_If_Invalid: Multiple unauthorised client contacts log conflicting requests; scope confusion
Customer_Action_Required: Designate and name primary AMS contact in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: Incident = unplanned interruption or degradation; distinguished from service requests and change requests; priority level determines SLA response
Risk_If_Invalid: Client logs everything as P1; SLA clock misapplied

Statement_Summary: Client classifies incident at logging; APPSolve may reclassify; downgrade notified with reason; disputes resolved by AMS Account Manager within 4 business hours
Risk_If_Invalid: Client disputes every reclassification; SLA clock manipulation

Statement_Summary: Incident ownership: APPSolve owns application config and integration defects; client data entry errors / business process errors / IT infrastructure / Oracle SaaS outages are not APPSolve defects
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve held responsible for client-caused data errors or Oracle platform outages

Statement_Summary: Oracle SaaS outages are outside APPSolve's control; APPSolve logs Oracle SRs and monitors; SLA clock paused during Oracle-caused outages
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve held to SLA during Oracle platform outage

Statement_Summary: For incidents requiring Oracle Support: APPSolve manages Oracle SR; resolution timeline outside APPSolve's control; weekly status updates provided
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to resolve Oracle bugs immediately regardless of Oracle SR timelines

Statement_Summary: Service request = planned pre-agreed config change within monthly hours; not a defect; not new scope; examples: new user / approval limit / GL account / supplier payment term
Risk_If_Invalid: Client logs enhancements as service requests to avoid CR process

Statement_Summary: Service requests prioritised within monthly allocation; APPSolve does not guarantee fulfilment of all SRs if volume exceeds allocation
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects unlimited service requests within fixed monthly fee
Customer_Action_Required: Prioritise service requests with client AMS contact

Statement_Summary: SRs requiring design / testing / stakeholder approval are raised as CRs; boundary between SR and CR is need for documented design and formal approval
Risk_If_Invalid: Simple CRs disguised as SRs to bypass CR process

Statement_Summary: Standard service request catalogue with pre-defined effort estimates; non-catalogue requests assessed per complexity and agreed before work commences
Risk_If_Invalid: Non-catalogue requests expected to be fulfilled at same speed as catalogue

Statement_Summary: Enhancements — new functionality / config / modules / integrations / reports beyond contracted scope — excluded from AMS; delivered as project work under separate SOW
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects new modules to be added without a separate project engagement

Statement_Summary: APPSolve identifies enhancement requests; logs them; advises client enhancement requires separate project; does not absorb into AMS hours
Risk_If_Invalid: Enhancement work absorbed into AMS without commercial agreement; margin eroded

Statement_Summary: APPSolve's obligation = identify when request crosses enhancement boundary; client decides whether and when to proceed
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve held responsible for not delivering an enhancement it identified as out of scope

Statement_Summary: Ambiguous requests escalated to AMS Account Manager and client designated contact for classification before work commences; agreed classification logged in monthly report
Risk_If_Invalid: Ambiguous requests started without classification causing scope dispute later
Customer_Action_Required: Agree classification process with AMS contact

Statement_Summary: CR = formally scoped and approved work item beyond standard SR catalogue; requires written scope / estimate / client approval; threshold for separate invoice vs monthly retainer defined in AMS agreement
Risk_If_Invalid: Every small config change raised as a CR even below threshold; admin burden
Customer_Action_Required: Agree CR threshold in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: CR process: client submits description > APPSolve estimates within 5 business days > client approves in writing > APPSolve delivers > client signs off; no CR work without written approval
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve proceeds with CR work based on verbal approval; client later disputes
Customer_Action_Required: Designate CR approval authority in writing

Statement_Summary: CRs not subject to incident SLA; delivery timeline agreed at CR approval based on complexity / resource availability / client priority
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects CR to be delivered within incident SLA timeframe

Statement_Summary: High-risk CRs (security roles / workflow restructuring / GL period-end config) assessed for risk before implementation; require test sign-off from client before production deployment
Risk_If_Invalid: High-risk config change deployed to production without testing; system issue
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm testing and sign-off process for high-risk CRs

Statement_Summary: Defect = system behaviour not conforming to agreed implementation design; resolved within AMS incident management / monthly hours at no additional charge
Risk_If_Invalid: Client disputes what constitutes a defect vs an enhancement
Customer_Action_Required: Retain implementation design documentation for defect boundary reference

Statement_Summary: Three-way classification: Defect (APPSolve resolves no charge) / Enhancement (separately priced project) / Configuration drift (APPSolve investigates; resolution billed as SR)
Risk_If_Invalid: Client argues all post-go-live issues are defects APPSolve must fix for free

Statement_Summary: Oracle quarterly update regressions managed as incidents within standard SLA; root cause (Oracle release) documented in monthly report; Oracle SRs logged where Oracle bug identified
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects Oracle quarterly update testing to be included in base AMS

Statement_Summary: Defects caused by client-initiated config changes without APPSolve involvement are treated as service requests; billed against monthly allocation
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to fix client-caused configuration errors at no charge as if they were defects
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge client-caused config issues billed as SRs

Statement_Summary: Standard AMS SLA defines response time targets per priority level; response time = time from ticket logging to APPSolve first substantive response; does not guarantee resolution time unless explicitly contracted
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects SLA to guarantee resolution not just response
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm SLA scope (response vs resolution) in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: SLA clock starts at ticket logging during business hours; outside-hours tickets start clock at 08:00 SAST next business day unless 24/7 SLA contracted
Risk_If_Invalid: Client argues SLA clock started when issue occurred not when ticket was logged
Customer_Action_Required: Require ticket logging as the SLA trigger event

Statement_Summary: SLA clock paused when: waiting for client information / Oracle SR pending / client has not approved required change / maintenance window in progress
Risk_If_Invalid: Client disputes SLA pause; claims APPSolve should resolve without client information

Statement_Summary: Monthly SLA performance report: tickets by priority / SLA compliance rate / breaches with root cause / Oracle SR status / CR status
Risk_If_Invalid: Client has no visibility of SLA performance; cannot assess value of AMS

Statement_Summary: Named consultant: APPSolve uses best-efforts scheduling for consistent assignment; not a contractual guarantee unless explicitly contracted as dedicated consultant arrangement
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects named consultant to be exclusively dedicated to their AMS
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm named vs team support model in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: P1 Critical (system outage / payroll blocked / financial close blocked / all integrations failed / data loss risk) / P2 High (major functionality impaired / critical process unable to proceed) / P3 Medium (non-critical impaired / workaround exists) / P4 Low (minor / how-to / non-urgent)
Risk_If_Invalid: Inconsistent priority classification between client and APPSolve; SLA disputes
Customer_Action_Required: Agree priority definitions in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: Priority escalation: client may request re-classification if business impact increases; AMS Account Manager reviews within 4 business hours
Risk_If_Invalid: Client unilaterally escalates all P3s to P1 without business justification

Statement_Summary: P1 outside business hours under standard SLA: APPSolve responds at start of next business day; 24/7 SLA required for out-of-hours P1 response
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects 24/7 P1 response under standard business hours AMS
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm P1 outside-hours handling in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: Oracle quarterly release advisory included in AMS: review release notes for contracted modules and flag changes with potential impact; regression testing NOT included in standard AMS unless specifically contracted
Risk_If_Invalid: Client assumes regression testing is included with release advisory
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm release testing scope in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: Oracle quarterly update regressions investigated and resolved as AMS incidents within standard SLA; Oracle bug → Oracle SR lodged and managed by APPSolve
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to prevent all Oracle update regressions

Statement_Summary: Full Oracle quarterly regression testing (systematic testing of all configured processes after each Oracle update) excluded from standard AMS; separately contracted service recommended for high-criticality environments
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects comprehensive regression testing included in standard AMS price
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge regression testing exclusion; contract separately if required

Statement_Summary: Acumatica release advisory included in Acumatica AMS: review release notes and flag customisation compatibility risks; Acumatica version upgrade execution excluded from standard AMS
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects Acumatica upgrades managed under standard AMS price
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge upgrade exclusion; raise separately if required

Statement_Summary: BeBanking update advisory included in BeBanking AMS; BeBanking-initiated format or connectivity changes may require OIC integration updates assessed per OIC pack; BeBanking platform monitoring is BeBanking's responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to manage BeBanking platform changes at no cost under AMS

Statement_Summary: Oracle Fusion HCM/ERP patches applied by Oracle as part of SaaS; APPSolve does not apply Oracle SaaS patches; APPSolve monitors for patch-related regressions
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to manage Oracle SaaS patching

Statement_Summary: Oracle EBS patching (on-premises or OCI-hosted) separately contracted; DBA task not included in standard application AMS unless DBA support component explicitly in scope
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects EBS patching included in application AMS
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm EBS patching scope in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: Acumatica maintenance builds applied by Acumatica or hosting partner; APPSolve monitors for build compatibility with client customisations and advises; build-caused customisation issues investigated and resolved under AMS
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects Acumatica builds to be managed and applied by APPSolve

Statement_Summary: Basic OIC integration monitoring (periodic review of OIC error console for active integration failures on contracted integrations) available as contracted AMS service item; not included in base AMS unless specifically contracted
Risk_If_Invalid: Client assumes OIC monitoring is included in all AMS agreements
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm monitoring requirement and frequency in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: Proactive monitoring (automated alerting / SLA dashboards / infrastructure monitoring / log aggregation) requires separate monitoring arrangement; APPSolve can configure Oracle OIC built-in alerting as a configuration activity
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects full monitoring platform provided by APPSolve under standard AMS
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge monitoring tool boundary; contract separately if required

Statement_Summary: Oracle Fusion HCM/ERP system health monitoring is Oracle's responsibility as SaaS provider; Oracle SaaS status page is authoritative for platform availability; APPSolve does not duplicate Oracle's infrastructure monitoring
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to monitor Oracle platform availability

Statement_Summary: BeBanking OIC integration error logs monitored by APPSolve where BeBanking AMS contracted; BeBanking platform monitoring (H2H portal / bank connectivity) is BeBanking's responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to monitor BeBanking platform itself

Statement_Summary: Monthly support report: support activity summary / SLA compliance / hours consumed vs allocation / open incidents and SRs / CRs / Oracle SR status / planned next month activities; within 5 business days of month-end
Risk_If_Invalid: Client has no visibility of support activity; unable to assess value for money

Statement_Summary: Monthly report delivered within 5 business days of calendar month-end via email to designated AMS contact and agreed distribution
Risk_If_Invalid: Late report delivery reduces client confidence in AMS service quality
Customer_Action_Required: Confirm report distribution list in AMS agreement

Statement_Summary: Quarterly AMS service review meeting (60–90 min remote) offered as standard; whether it takes place is at client's discretion
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects formal quarterly governance meeting included in all AMS agreements

Statement_Summary: Ad hoc AMS reports on request fulfilled within monthly hours allocation (annual CR summary / audit support extract / period-specific activity)
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects ad hoc reports immediately at no cost beyond monthly fee

Statement_Summary: Client owns business processes; functional leads make process change decisions / document requirements / approve solutions before APPSolve implements; APPSolve does not redesign processes under AMS
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to redesign client business processes under AMS
Customer_Action_Required: Maintain active functional team engagement under AMS

Statement_Summary: Client's internal helpdesk/ERP team provides Level 1 support (how-to / password resets / existing functionality explanation / issue triage) unless Level 1 explicitly contracted to APPSolve
Risk_If_Invalid: All user queries routed directly to APPSolve including basic user training
Customer_Action_Required: Establish and maintain internal Level 1 support function

Statement_Summary: User account creation / role assignment / user deactivation are client IT team's responsibility unless explicitly contracted as part of AMS
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to manage all Oracle user accounts under standard AMS
Customer_Action_Required: Allocate internal resource for user access management

Statement_Summary: Internal change management (communicating system changes to users / updating job aids / managing internal change) is client's responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to communicate system changes to all users under AMS

Statement_Summary: All CRs approved in writing by designated AMS contact or named delegate before work commences; verbal approval not sufficient
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve proceeds with CR work based on verbal approval; client later disputes estimate or invoice
Customer_Action_Required: Designate and empower AMS contact to approve CRs

Statement_Summary: Client functional team performs and signs off tests before production deployment of CR / SR / incident resolution; APPSolve deploys to production only after written sign-off
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to test on behalf of client; then client disputes production outcome
Customer_Action_Required: Allocate tester availability for all configuration changes requiring test sign-off

Statement_Summary: Client provides accurate and complete information when logging tickets (error messages / screenshots / steps to reproduce / affected users / business impact); incomplete information delays investigation
Risk_If_Invalid: SLA clock runs while APPSolve waits for client information; SLA breach attributed to APPSolve
Customer_Action_Required: Train AMS users on quality ticket logging

Statement_Summary: Network / hardware / browser / VPN / printer / IT infrastructure are client IT team's responsibility; Oracle SaaS infrastructure is Oracle's; APPSolve AMS covers application config layer only
Risk_If_Invalid: Client routes infrastructure issues to APPSolve

Statement_Summary: Client provides APPSolve with Oracle CSI (Customer Support Identifier) access to log Oracle SRs on client's behalf; without CSI access APPSolve provides SR description and evidence for client to log
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve cannot manage Oracle SRs without CSI access; SLA for Oracle issues deteriorates
Customer_Action_Required: Grant APPSolve CSI access at AMS onboarding

Statement_Summary: Client provides AMS onboarding documentation: implementation project documentation / current system access credentials / AMS contact introductions; where APPSolve implemented the system this documentation already exists
Risk_If_Invalid: Onboarding delayed due to missing documentation; first month's support ineffective
Customer_Action_Required: Prepare onboarding documentation pack before AMS start date

Statement_Summary: New module implementations excluded from AMS; delivered as project work under separate implementation SOW
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects new Oracle/Acumatica module to be delivered under AMS at no additional cost

Statement_Summary: New OIC integration or BeBanking integration development excluded from AMS; governed by OIC pack for integration development assumptions
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects new integrations to be developed under AMS

Statement_Summary: New data migration post-go-live (newly acquired company / merged entity) excluded from AMS; requires separately scoped data migration engagement
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects new entity's data to be loaded under standard AMS

Statement_Summary: New report development excluded from AMS; adjusting existing in-scope reports is a service request; new report development is a project item
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects unlimited new reports under AMS

Statement_Summary: Regulatory compliance advice (tax / legal / audit) excluded from AMS; config to implement compliance requirements must be driven by client-provided spec approved by client tax/legal team
Risk_If_Invalid: APPSolve expected to advise on tax treatment or IFRS interpretation under AMS

Statement_Summary: End-user training excluded from standard AMS; separately contracted training engagement; APPSolve can provide quick reference cards for simple how-to queries within monthly hours
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects formal training sessions delivered under AMS
Customer_Action_Required: Acknowledge training exclusion; contract separately if required

Statement_Summary: Network / hardware / browser / VPN / printer / IT infrastructure support excluded; Oracle SaaS infrastructure is Oracle's; APPSolve AMS = application config layer only
Risk_If_Invalid: Client routes IT infrastructure tickets to APPSolve AMS

Statement_Summary: New Acumatica customisation development (low-code extensions / workflow customisations / custom web services / custom report designer) excluded from standard Acumatica AMS
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects new Acumatica customisations delivered under AMS

Statement_Summary: Major version upgrades (Oracle EBS / Acumatica / BeBanking platform) excluded from standard AMS; project-level activities requiring separate planning and delivery engagement
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects major upgrades managed under standard AMS

Statement_Summary: Oracle licensing and subscription management excluded from AMS; APPSolve advises on licensing implications; does not purchase/renew/modify Oracle licences on client's behalf
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to manage Oracle licence renewals under AMS

Statement_Summary: Oracle Analytics Cloud (OAX/FAW) dashboard and data model development excluded from AMS; OAX development is separately scoped project work
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects OAX report development under standard AMS price

Statement_Summary: Disaster recovery testing excluded from AMS; Oracle SaaS DR is Oracle's responsibility; on-premises DR is client IT team's responsibility
Risk_If_Invalid: Client expects APPSolve to manage DR testing for Oracle ERP under AMS
